~~~text
# Session-API Line-Count Audit Handoff

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

- `AUDIT-TICKET` = [d8cb1b87 [session-api][audit] Remediate default file-length findings](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d8cb1b87-48a2-4a99-b741-48cfaed44711/ticket.toml)
- `HANDOFF` = persisted handoff `e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546`
- `SESSION` = durable workspace session `03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f`
- `RUN` = outgoing session run `52e1cdc9-d4b9-4c8d-8316-615151b909c8`

Resume the remaining `session-api` file-length remediation from `memory-api/`. `AUDIT-TICKET` is already `in-implementation` and has no active board ownership. The most recent captured audit selector used a 1,000-line threshold and identified two remaining violations; the ticket’s actual completion gate is stricter: the unparameterized default audit uses `max_file_lines = 400`.

## Durable Session Identity

- `workspace_session_id`: `03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f`
- outgoing `run_id`: `52e1cdc9-d4b9-4c8d-8316-615151b909c8`
- `handoff_id`: `e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546`
- Exact resume command:
  `session-cli resume --workspace-session-id 03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f --predecessor-run-id 52e1cdc9-d4b9-4c8d-8316-615151b909c8`
- Persisted handoff artifact: [e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546.json](.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/e579cb4f-39ce-4137-b744-e932fa919546.json)

## Key Entities

- `AUDIT-TICKET`: [d8cb1b87 [session-api][audit] Remediate default file-length findings](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d8cb1b87-48a2-4a99-b741-48cfaed44711/ticket.toml) is the implementation tracker and defines the default-400-line acceptance gate.
- [AUDIT-TICKET scope and validation contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d8cb1b87-48a2-4a99-b741-48cfaed44711/description.md) explains the historical threshold confusion and records the required validation commands.
- [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) is 1,034 lines under the 1,000-line audit and contains transcript parsing, event-envelope construction, event dispatch, and capture-record conversion.
- [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) is 1,156 lines under the 1,000-line audit and contains explicit-ingestion extraction, failed-tool mapping, feedback-entry recovery, and unit tests.
- [hook tests](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs) exercise the hook parser and are the immediate behavioral regression surface.
- [feedback-ingestion ticket](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5/ticket.toml) documents the prior event-based ingestion behavior and focused `transcript_feedback` validation; preserve that public behavior while splitting modules.
- [hook-capture ticket](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b3155a94-230e-416b-be0e-5948d6d2193a/ticket.toml) records passing hook E2E coverage and is useful context for externally visible capture behavior.

## Findings And Decisions

- The prior captured audit report, filtered to `crates/session-api/` with `line_count > 1000`, reported:
  - [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs): 1,034 lines, threshold 1,000.
  - [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs): 1,156 lines, threshold 1,000.
- `AUDIT-TICKET`’s description is stale for `hook.rs`: it says 726 lines. Update its implementation/validation summary with the actual post-refactor audit result rather than treating that historical list as current.
- Do not use the 1,000-line selector as the definition of done. The ticket explicitly corrects the rule: run the canonical, unparameterized audit against `memory-api`; no `session-api` file may remain above the default 400-line limit.
- Prefer extraction over behavioral rewrites:
  - [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) already has a `tool_execution` child module. Likely extractions are event-envelope types and parsing/normalization helpers into cohesive private submodules while preserving the existing public re-exports from `session_api`.
  - [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) already separates `entity_discovery`, `event_outcomes`, and `turn_signals`; extract the large failed-tool mapping policy and/or recovery logic next, preserving public API locations through re-exports as needed.
- The baseline compile check completed successfully:
  `rtk cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml --no-run`.
- Rendering the root durable session’s pinned instructions is currently blocked by stale pin `ce://default/rule/84fa9769-cff9-4d89-9068-88474584b4b3`, which no longer resolves. This does not block the Rust refactor; resume the session and either repair/remove that pin through the appropriate rule/session workflow or record it as a separate bootstrap defect.

## Board And Worktree Notes

- The root board has one stale entry for unrelated feedback-MCP work; it owns only `memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/**` and an unrelated spec. It does not conflict with `session-api`.
- `memory-api` was clean on branch `main` when this handoff was created.
- The root worktree has unrelated modifications, including `.gitignore`, a root spec body, the `memory-api` submodule pointer, and untracked `.session/sessions/**`. Do not revert, stage, or modify them for this audit track.
- Before editing, check out `AUDIT-TICKET` on the memory-api board and claim only the source/test files actually selected for the first split.

## Next Actions

1. Resume `SESSION` using the exact command above, `cd memory-api`, inspect `AUDIT-TICKET`, then check it out and claim the first selected file set.
2. Run the canonical audit against `memory-api` with no `max_file_lines` override. This is the first discriminating check: it establishes the live default-400 queue and confirms whether the two 1,000-line findings are still the only remaining work in this track.
3. Read the local portions of [hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) and [hook tests](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs), choose one cohesive extraction that reduces the file materially without changing public API, and make that smallest split.
4. Immediately run `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib`. Repair only failures attributable to that extraction and rerun the same command.
5. Repeat for [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs), retaining event-based mining, exact mapping policy, and recovery semantics. Run the focused relevant test selector first when practical, then `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib`.
6. Rerun the canonical unparameterized audit. Continue splitting any `session-api` file still over 400 lines until the audit has no `session-api` `line_count` findings.
7. Update `AUDIT-TICKET` with final source-file list, exact audit evidence, test result, any remaining blocker, and move it to `in-review` only after the required gate passes.

Execute these actions in order and continue until every action is completed or explicitly blocked.

## Completion Status

- Resume durable session: `not-started` - exact lineage and handoff record are available.
- Check out and claim `AUDIT-TICKET`: `not-started` - no relevant active ownership exists.
- Establish live default-audit queue: `not-started` - prior evidence includes a custom 1,000-line selector, while acceptance is 400 lines.
- Split and validate `hook.rs`: `not-started` - current measured size is 1,034 lines.
- Split and validate `transcript_feedback.rs`: `not-started` - current measured size is 1,156 lines.
- Canonical audit closure and ticket update: `not-started` - required after all splits.
- Stale durable-rule pin repair: `blocked` - the unresolved rule pin prevents focused instruction rendering but is not a blocker for the audit implementation.

## Definition Of Done

`AUDIT-TICKET` is ready for review when `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` passes after each implementation slice, the canonical unparameterized audit against `memory-api` reports no `session-api` `line_count` finding above 400 lines, public behavior/re-exports remain compatible, and the ticket contains the final audit and validation evidence.
~~~
